subject: [PATCH 2/4] mm: Rename node_get_allowed_targets() to make it more explicit

reviews.author: Joshua Hahn
reviews.summary: Reviewer suggested renaming node_get_allowed_targets() to something more explicit and agreed with the original patch, but thought the new name could be shortened further.
reviews.raw_body:
Agreed, node_get_allowed_targets is pretty vague ; -)

I do think that node_get_allowed_demotion_targets could be considered
a bit too long, but I don't think it's called in too many places where
there would be more than just this function call in the line so LGTM!

Please feel free to add my review tag, have a great day Alexandre!

Reviewed-by: Joshua Hahn <joshua.hahnjy@gmail.com>

reviews.author: Byungchul Park
reviews.summary: Reviewer Byungchul Park suggested renaming the function to node_get_demotion_targets() instead of node_get_allowed_demotion_targets(), finding it more intuitive.
reviews.raw_body:
Look better than before to me.

What about just node_get_demotion_targets()?

	Byungchul

reviews.author: Donet Tom
reviews.summary: Reviewer Donet Tom approved the patch, giving it a 'Reviewed-by' tag.
reviews.raw_body:
This looks LGTM

Reviewed by: Donet Tom <donettom@linux.ibm.com>

reviews.author: David (Arm)
reviews.summary: Reviewer David suggested renaming node_get_allowed_demotion_targets() to mt_get_node_demotion_targets() for consistency with other functions in the memory-tiers API, and gave a positive review (+1) indicating approval of the patch.
reviews.raw_body:
+1

Maybe throw in the mt_ prefix and call it

mt_get_node_demotion_targets()

-- 
Cheers,

David

reviews.author: Alexandre Ghiti (author)
reviews.summary:
Author acknowledged that the function name change was not properly reflected in all instances and agreed to restructure in v2.

Author agreed to rename the function in a future version of the patch, specifically v2.
reviews.raw_body:
Hi David, Byungchul,

On 3/12/26 13:58, David Hildenbrand (Arm) wrote:

---

I'll do, thanks

Alex

2026-03-11.patch_summary: This patch renames the function `node_get_allowed_targets()` to `node_get_allowed_demotion_targets()` in the Linux kernel's memory management code. The original function name was misleading, as it actually returns tier nodes targeted during demotion, not allowed targets. This change aims to make the function name more explicit and accurate. No functional changes are intended by this patch.
